org.arakhne.neteditor.swing

Class JFigureView<G extends Graph<?,?,?,?>>

  • java.lang.Object
    • java.awt.Component
      • java.awt.Container
        • javax.swing.JComponent
          • javax.swing.JPanel
            • org.arakhne.afc.ui.swing.zoom.ZoomableView
              • org.arakhne.neteditor.swing.JFigureView<G>
  • Type Parameters:
    G - is the type of the graph supported by this panel.
    All Implemented Interfaces:
    ImageObserver, MenuContainer, Printable, Serializable, Iterable<Figure>, Accessible, ZoomableContext, ViewComponentContainer<Figure,G>

    public class JFigureView<G extends Graph<?,?,?,?>>
extends ZoomableView
implements ViewComponentContainer<Figure,G>
    This class provides a viewer for manipulating graphical network The editor (JFigurePanel) is the central class of the graph editing framework. All the net-level models, graphical objects, layers, editor modes, editor commands, and supporting dialogs and frames are implemented in their own classes.

    Version:
    17.0 2013-10-23 21:03:09
    Author:
    Mahdi HANNOUN, Stéphane GALLAND, Marc BAUMGARTNER
    See Also:
    Serialized Form
    Maven Group Id:
    org.arakhne.neteditor
    Maven Artifact Id:
    neteditor-swingpanel

      Add listener on exceptions.
      int addFigure(Figure component)
      Add the element at the specified index.
      void addFigureListener(FigureListener listener)
      Add listener on figure events.
      protected Rectangle2f calcViewBounds()
      Compute and reply the bounds of the entire document.
      (package private) void closePopupEditor()
      Cancel and close the popup editor.
      void copy()
      Transfers the currently selected figures to the system clipboard, leaving the contents in the text model.
      void cut()
      Transfers the currently selected figures to the system clipboard, removing the contents from the model.
      void fireError(Throwable error)
      Notifies the listeners about an error that occurs in one of the JFigureEditor components.
      protected void fireFigureAdded(Figure added)
      Notifies the listeners about the addition of a figure.
      protected void fireFigureChanged(Figure changed)
      Notifies the listeners about the change of a figure.
      protected void fireFigureRemoved(Figure removed)
      Notifies the listeners about the removal of a figure.
      Color getBackgroundColor()
      Replies the background color of the panel.
      CollisionAvoider getCollisionAvoider()
      Replies the object that permits to avoid collision between a figure to insert inside and the already present figures.
      Figure getFigureAt(float x, float y)
      Replies the figure that has the specified point in its shape.
      Figure getFigureAt(int index)
      Replies the element at the specified index.
      int getFigureCount()
      Replies the number of components in this container.
      FigureFactory<G> getFigureFactory()
      Replies the figure factory used by this panel.
      Figure getFigureIn(Rectangle2f bounds)
      Replies the first figure that are inside the specified bounds.
      Figure getFigureOn(Shape2f bounds)
      Replies the first figure that are intersecting the specified bounds.
      Collection<? extends Figure> getFigures()
      Replies the figures in the container.
      Set<Figure> getFiguresIn(Rectangle2f bounds)
      Replies the figures that are inside the specified bounds.
      Set<Figure> getFiguresOn(Shape2f bounds)
      Replies the figures that are intersecting the specified bounds.
      Figure getFigureWithBoundsAt(float x, float y)
      Replies the figu with the precision re that has its bounds with the specified point inside.

      Replies the graph displayed in this viewer.

      Replies the precision of the clicks (in pixels).

      Replies the action mode manager used by this viewser.

      Replies the background color used for the selection of objects.

      Replies the background color associated to selection.

      Replies the foreground color used for the selection of objects.

      Replies the foreground color associated to selection.

      Replies the type of graph supported by this viewer.

      Relies the id of the view.

      Replies the bounds of the view of the components.

      Import the specified figure into this editor and replies an undoable edit.

      Import the graph and the specified figures into this editor.

      Replies the index of the figure.

      Replies if the axis are drawn.

      Replies if this viewer is interactively editable.

      Replies if the figure are automatically added into the view when a model object was added in the graph.

      • deletionLock

        Lock resource that may be used for deletion actions.

    • Constructor Detail

      • JFigureView

        public JFigureView(Class<G> supportedGraphType,
           G g,
           FigureFactory<G> figureFactory,
           boolean isAlwaysRemovingModelObjects)
        Construct a new JFigurePanel.
        Parameters:
        supportedGraphType - is the type supported by this viewer.
        g - is the graph to display.
        figureFactory - is the factory for figures.
        isAlwaysRemovingModelObjects - is true to allow this base mode to remove the model objects each time an associated figure is removed; false to permits to the user to select the figure or the model object to be removed.

    • Method Detail

      • isAlwaysRemovingModelObjects

        public boolean isAlwaysRemovingModelObjects()
        Replies if the mode container want to remove the model objects when a figure is removed from the view.
        Returns:
        true if both the figure and the model objects should be removed at the same time, false if only the figure should be removed (not the model object).

        public final Class<G> getSupportedGraphType()
        Replies the type of graph supported by this viewer.

      • setClickPrecision

        public void setClickPrecision(float precision)
        Set the precision of the clicks (in pixels). Byt default, the value of CLICK_PRECISION is used.

      • isFigureAutomaticallyAdded

        public boolean isFigureAutomaticallyAdded()
        Replies if the figure are automatically added into the view when a model object was added in the graph. The figures are created with the figure factory replied by getFigureFactory().
        Returns:
        true if the figures are automatically added; otherwise false.

      • setFigureAutomaticallyAdded

        public void setFigureAutomaticallyAdded(boolean auto)
        Replies if the figure are automatically added into the view when a model object was added in the graph. The figures are created with the figure factory replied by getFigureFactory().
        Parameters:
        auto - is true if the figures are automatically added; otherwise false.

      • addFigure

        public int addFigure(Figure component)
        Add the element at the specified index.
        Specified by:
        addFigure in interface ViewComponentContainer<Figure,G extends Graph<?,?,?,?>>
        Parameters:
        component - is the component to add.
        Returns:
        the position where the figure is inserted; or -1 if the figure was not added.
        See Also:
        importFigure(DecorationFigure), importGraph(String, Graph, Map)

      • removeFigure

        public int removeFigure(Figure component)
        Remove the specified element. This function also unlink the model object associated to the figure.
        Specified by:
        removeFigure in interface ViewComponentContainer<Figure,G extends Graph<?,?,?,?>>
        Parameters:
        component - is the component to remove.
        Returns:
        the position of the removed figure.
        See Also:
        removeFigure(Figure, boolean)

      • removeFigure

        public int removeFigure(Figure component,
               boolean unlinkToModelObject)
        Remove the specified component.
        Parameters:
        component - is the component to remove.
        unlinkToModelObject - indicates if the removed component should be unlink to its model object.
        Returns:
        position of the removed figure; or -1 if not found.
        See Also:
        removeFigure(Figure)

        public Figure removeFigureAt(int index)

        Specified by:
        removeFigureAt in interface ViewComponentContainer<Figure,G extends Graph<?,?,?,?>>
        Parameters:
        index - is the position of the element to remove.
        Returns:
        the removed figure.

        paintViewComponents in interface ViewComponentContainer<Figure,G extends Graph<?,?,?,?>>
        Parameters:
        g - the graphic context.

      • paintOutsideGrayed

        protected void paintOutsideGrayed(ZoomableGraphics2D g)
        Paint the outside of the workspace with gray.
        Parameters:
        g -

      • paintAxis

        protected void paintAxis(ZoomableGraphics2D g)
        Paint the axis.
        Parameters:
        g -

      • repaint

        public void repaint(Figure figure)
        Repaint the graphical area around the specified figure. If the specified object is null the entire area is repainted.
        Parameters:
        figure -

      • repaint

        public void repaint(ModelObject obj)
        Repaint the graphical area around the figures associated to the specified object. If the specified object is null the entire area is repainted.
        Parameters:
        obj -

      • calcViewBounds

        protected Rectangle2f calcViewBounds()
        Compute and reply the bounds of the entire document.
        Returns:
        the bounds of the entire document.

      • getViewBounds

        public final Rectangle2f getViewBounds()
        Replies the bounds of the view of the components.
        Specified by:
        getViewBounds in interface ViewComponentContainer<Figure,G extends Graph<?,?,?,?>>
        Returns:
        the bounds of the view of the components.

      • getFigures

        public Collection<? extends Figure> getFigures()
        Replies the figures in the container.
        Returns:
        the figures.

      • getFigureAt

        public Figure getFigureAt(float x,
                 float y)
        Replies the figure that has the specified point in its shape. If you want to test the point against the bounds of the figures, please use getFigureWithBoundsAt(float, float). This function uses the precision of the clicks, which is replied by getHitPrecision().
        Parameters:
        x -
        y -
        Returns:
        the hit figure, or null if none.
        See Also:
        getFigureWithBoundsAt(float, float)

      • getFigureWithBoundsAt

        public Figure getFigureWithBoundsAt(float x,
                           float y)
        Replies the figu with the precision re that has its bounds with the specified point inside. If you want to test the point against the shape of the figures, please use getFigureAt(float, float). This function uses the precision of the clicks, which is replied by getHitPrecision().
        Parameters:
        x -
        y -
        Returns:
        the hit figure, or null if none.

      • getFiguresOn

        public Set<Figure> getFiguresOn(Shape2f bounds)
        Replies the figures that are intersecting the specified bounds.
        Parameters:
        bounds -
        Returns:
        the hit figures, never null.

      • getFiguresIn

        public Set<Figure> getFiguresIn(Rectangle2f bounds)
        Replies the figures that are inside the specified bounds.
        Parameters:
        bounds -
        Returns:
        the hit figures, never null.

      • getFigureOn

        public Figure getFigureOn(Shape2f bounds)
        Replies the first figure that are intersecting the specified bounds.
        Parameters:
        bounds -
        Returns:
        the hit figure, or null.

      • getFigureIn

        public Figure getFigureIn(Rectangle2f bounds)
        Replies the first figure that are inside the specified bounds.
        Parameters:
        bounds -
        Returns:
        the hit figure, or null.

      • moveFigureUp

        public void moveFigureUp(Figure figure)
        Move the figure up in the layers used by this panel.
        Parameters:
        figure - is the figure to move up.

      • moveFigureDown

        public void moveFigureDown(Figure figure)
        Move the figure down in the layers used by this panel.
        Parameters:
        figure - is the figure to move down.

      • moveFigureFront

        public void moveFigureFront(Figure figure)
        Move the figure at the front in the layers used by this panel.
        Parameters:
        figure - is the figure to move to the front.

      • moveFigureBackground

        public void moveFigureBackground(Figure figure)
        Move the figure at the background in the layers used by this panel.
        Parameters:
        figure - is the figure to move to the background.

      • moveFigureAt

        public int moveFigureAt(Figure figure,
               int index)
        Move the figure at the specified position in the layers used by this panel.
        Parameters:
        figure - is the figure to move to the front.
        index - is the new position of the figure.
        Returns:
        the previous position of the figure; or -1 if the figure was not found.
        Throws:
        IllegalArgumentException

      • indexOfFigure

        public int indexOfFigure(Figure figure)
        Replies the index of the figure. This index is also the position of the from the background.
        Parameters:
        figure - is the figure to search for.
        Returns:
        the position of the figure; -1 if is was not found.

      • importGraph

        public Undoable importGraph(String undoLabel,
                   G graph,
                   Map<UUID,List<ViewComponent>> figures)
        Import the graph and the specified figures into this editor.
        Parameters:
        undoLabel - is the label of the undoable edit to reply.
        graph - is the graph to integrate.
        figures - are the figures (classified per view id) to import, from front to background.
        Returns:
        the undoable edit that permits to undo the import.

      • importGraph

        public Undoable importGraph(String undoLabel,
                   G graph,
                   List<ViewComponent> figures)
        Import the graph and the specified figures into this editor.
        Parameters:
        undoLabel - is the label of the undoable edit to reply.
        graph - is the graph to integrate.
        figures - are the figures (classified per view id) to import, from front to background.
        Returns:
        the undoable edit that permits to undo the import.

      • importFigure

        public Undoable importFigure(DecorationFigure figure)
        Import the specified figure into this editor and replies an undoable edit.
        Parameters:
        figure - is the figure to import.
        Returns:
        the undoable edit that permits to undo the import.

      • fireError

        public void fireError(Throwable error)
        Notifies the listeners about an error that occurs in one of the JFigureEditor components.
        Parameters:
        error -

      • addExceptionListener

        public void addExceptionListener(ExceptionListener listener)
        Add listener on exceptions.
        Parameters:
        listener -

      • removeExceptionListener

        public void removeExceptionListener(ExceptionListener listener)
        Remove listener on exceptions. If there is no listener on exceptions or all the listeners are replying false, the default catching behavior is executed: the exception is forwaded to the VM.
        Parameters:
        listener -

      • getSelectionManager

        public JSelectionManager getSelectionManager()
        Replies the selection manager.
        Returns:
        the selection manager.

      • getUndoManager

        public UndoManagerSwing getUndoManager()
        Replies the manager of undoable actions.
        Returns:
        the undo manager.

      • getModeManager

        public ActionModeManager getModeManager()
        Replies the action mode manager used by this viewser.
        Returns:
        the manager.

      • cut

        public void cut()
        Transfers the currently selected figures to the system clipboard, removing the contents from the model. The current selection is reset. Does nothing for null selections.

      • copy

        public void copy()
        Transfers the currently selected figures to the system clipboard, leaving the contents in the text model. The current selection remains intact. Does nothing for null selections.

      • paste

        public void paste()
        Transfers the contents of the system clipboard into the associated graph. If the clipboard is empty, does nothing.

      • openPopupEditor

        void openPopupEditor(TextFigure figure,
                   Point2D mousePosition)
        Open the popup editor for the specified figure.
        Parameters:
        figure -
        mousePosition - is the position of the mouse when opening the editor; may be null if the mouse position is unknown.

      • closePopupEditor

        void closePopupEditor()
        Cancel and close the popup editor.
